rules.name: RWX Memory Allocation in System Process

false_positive_disproval.description: Verify if the process is a known JIT compiler. If so, RWX memory may be expected.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Process is known JIT THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the process is a baseline Windows executable (e.g., MsMpEng.exe, SearchApp.exe). If it is a system process but contains unbacked RWX regions with jump tables or INT3 padding, it is highly suspicious and fails disproval.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Process IN baseline_processes AND Disasm contains anomalies THEN FAIL_TO_DISPROVE.

mitre_mapping.procedure: Process Injection: RWX shellcode in system process

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the process name truncation is an artifact of 16-bit DOS naming or known legitimate software.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Process name is a known historical standard THEN DISPROVE.

rules.name: Staging Archive in Temporary or User Directory

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the archive is a scheduled IT backup or known administrative task.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF archive name matches corporate backup schema THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Verify if the user is authorized to access the destination drive/folder and if the action aligns with normal business operations.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF User is authorized AND destination is a corporate network share THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the script is part of a legitimate MSI installer or software updater.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Script is known software updater THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Verify if the binary belongs to a legitimate self-extracting application or installer.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF Binary is a known benign installer THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the secure wipe was performed during authorized decommissioning or normal user cleanup.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF action performed by IT Admin during decommission THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Verify if the autorun entry belongs to digitally signed baseline software.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF registry path points to signed standard software THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: CRITICAL: You MUST set request_deep_carve to true to extract strings and verify the presence of USBSTOR keys. If you haven't received carved strings yet, set request_deep_carve=true. If carved strings show USBSTOR or removable media identifiers, FAIL to disprove.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF USB device is corporate issued AND user is authorized THEN DISPROVE.

false_positive_disproval.description: Check if the LNK file points to an explicitly authorized corporate backup drive. The evaluator must strictly verify the destination drive letter against authorized parameters (e.g. network shares). External USBs (e.g. E: or F:) are unauthorized and FAIL to disprove.
false_positive_disproval.disproval_logic: IF LNK points to an explicitly authorized backup drive THEN DISPROVE.
